2025-01-01.title: A Mayor’s Odyssey: From Undocumented Migrant to Cartel Target

2025-01-25.abstract: The young women freed on Saturday were working as “spotters” for Israel’s army when they were abducted in the Hamas-led attack on Oct. 7, 2023.

2025-01-25.title: Europe Is Pouring Money Into Defense. Is It Enough?
2025-01-25.abstract: With a full-blown land war in Ukraine and an isolationist American president back in the White House, European leaders have come to a stark conclusion: They must spend more on their militaries.

2025-01-26.title: Trump Says He Wants Jordan and Egypt to Take in Palestinians From Gaza
2025-01-26.abstract: President Trump said he had spoken to Jordan’s leader and planned to call Egypt’s on Sunday. Most of Gaza’s two million residents have been displaced in 15 months of fighting.

2025-01-26.title: Here’s the latest.
2025-01-26.abstract: Any prolonged Israeli occupation of southern Lebanon could breathe new life into Hezbollah, a group that was founded to liberate Lebanon from Israeli occupation and has portrayed itself as the only force capable of protecting Lebanon’s borders, experts say. It also threatens to derail the current political momentum in Lebanon, where for the

2025-01-26.title: India, a Big Source of Illegal Migration, Hopes to Navigate the Trump Storm
2025-01-26.abstract: Fear and uncertainty are rippling through India, which sends more undocumented migrants to the United States than any other country outside Latin America.

2025-01-26.title: Monuments in Australia Are Vandalized to Protest National Day
2025-01-26.abstract: The vandalism unfolded in Sydney, Melbourne and Canberra. Australia Day is a divisive holiday that critics see as a symbol of racism and oppression.

2025-01-26.title: A Hardened Detective and an Angry Rock Star: How a Vast Art Fraud Was Cracked
2025-01-26.abstract: Two art fraud rings in a remote Canadian city produced thousands of paintings sold in galleries as works by Norval Morrisseau, Canada’s most celebrated Indigenous artist.

2025-01-27.title: Israeli Army Raids Home of Freed Hamas Member Amid Tensions Over Hostage Deal
2025-01-27.abstract: Soldiers disrupted a Jerusalem gathering to mark the early release of an imprisoned Palestinian, arresting his brother and roughing up his father. A Times reporter conducting interviews was also assaulted.

2025-01-27.title: As Trump and Putin Circle Each Other, an Agenda Beyond Ukraine Emerges
2025-01-27.abstract: President Trump jabs at the Russian leader with threats; Vladimir Putin responds with flattery. But there are notable signals in their jousting, including a revived discussion about nuclear arms control.

2025-01-27.title: China’s Large and Mysterious Dam Project Is Alarming Neighbors and Experts
2025-01-27.abstract: The hydropower dam, in quake-prone Tibet, is set to be the world’s biggest. But China has said little about the project, which could affect nearby countries.

2025-01-27.title: At Auschwitz, a Solemn Ceremony at a Time of Rising Nationalism
2025-01-27.abstract: World leaders and a dwindling group of survivors joined in a ceremony to mark the 80th anniversary of the liberation of the Nazi death camp by the Red Army.

2025-01-28.title: Inside Colombia’s Crisis Over Trump’s Deportations
2025-01-28.abstract: When Colombia’s president, Gustavo Petro, refused military planes carrying deportees, infuriating President Trump, he revealed how heated the question of deportations has become.

2025-01-28.title: Caution Ahead! Vietnam’s Drivers Are Suddenly Following the Rules.
2025-01-28.abstract: Steep new fines — more than many people make in a month — have made the streets of Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi less freewheeling than they used to be.

2025-01-28.title: India and China Agree to Resume Direct Flights After Nearly Five Years
2025-01-28.abstract: It was the latest thaw in relations between the two countries, whose troops were involved in deadly skirmishes high in the Himalayas in 2020.

2025-01-28.title: Lebanon Faces a Colossal Disposal Task: Clearing War Debris
2025-01-28.abstract: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah has left behind lots of rubble. Some experts fear that much of it will be dumped into the environment without controls.

2025-01-28.title: ‘The Gulf of America’? Here’s What Mexicans and Cubans Think.
2025-01-28.abstract: Following President Trump’s order renaming the Gulf of Mexico, at least in the United States, Mexicans and Cubans expressed annoyance, defiance, confusion and even amusement.

2025-01-29.title: Israel Is Urged to Reverse Course on Palestinian Aid Agency Ban
2025-01-29.abstract: Diplomats at the United Nations Security Council said planned restrictions on the agency, UNRWA, would imperil Palestinians. Only the U.S. sides with Israel.

2025-01-29.title: How Extreme Weather Scrambled a Lunar New Year Tradition in Vietnam
2025-01-29.abstract: A typhoon and extreme heat dramatically affected prices for the kumquat trees that are ubiquitous during the holiday.

2025-01-29.title: At Least 30 Dead and Many More Hurt in Stampede at Huge Hindu Festival in India
2025-01-29.abstract: People were trampled as pilgrims at the Maha Kumbh Mela, one of the world’s biggest gatherings, gathered where the Ganges and Yamuna Rivers meet, officials said.

2025-01-29.title: Fireworks and Family Feasts Kick Off Year of the Snake
2025-01-29.abstract: More than a billion people around the world are celebrating the Lunar New Year.

2025-01-29.title: Citizen Scientists Reclaim Japan’s Nuclear Disaster Zone
2025-01-29.abstract: Armed with measuring devices, groups of citizens are embracing science to monitor radioactive fallout — and regain control of lives upended by the 2011 meltdowns in Fukushima.

2025-01-30.title: A U.K. Teen Became Fixated on Extreme Violence. But Was It Terrorism?
2025-01-30.abstract: Axel Rudakubana, who killed three girls in a stabbing spree at a dance class, was obsessed with violence but had no known ideology. His case has led to calls for a rethink of counterterror strategy.

2025-01-30.title: Here’s the latest on the hostage and prisoner exchange.
A Paris Theater, Occupied by Migrants Hoping to Prove They’re Just Kids
2025-01-30.abstract: More than 300 homeless African immigrants are camping out in the Gaîté Lyrique, waiting for officials to rule on whether they are minors and eligible for benefits like housing.

2025-01-30.title: Trump Calls Canada a Big Player in the Fentanyl Trade. Is It?
2025-01-30.abstract: President Trump has threatened to impose tariffs on Canada over drug trafficking, equating it with Mexico. U.S. government reports do not support the claim.

2025-01-30.title: Trump’s Foreign Aid Freeze Causes Fear of H.I.V. Resurgence in Africa
2025-01-30.abstract: Patients and health care advocates said the abrupt decision to halt U.S. funding for a lifesaving H.I.V. program led to widespread confusion. The backtracking didn’t help.

2025-01-31.title: North Korean Troops No Longer Seen on Front Lines Fighting Ukraine
2025-01-31.abstract: North Korea sent its best troops to aid Russia in its war against Ukraine. But after months of suffering severe losses, they have been taken off the front line.

2025-01-31.title: Kremlin Chokes YouTube Service, but Russians Find Ways Around It
2025-01-31.abstract: The Kremlin is trying to cripple YouTube in Russia, internet experts say, pushing some people to state-controlled domestic alternatives. But many Russians have found workarounds.

2025-01-31.title: Why Are These Motorcycle Taxi Drivers Wearing Pink?
2025-01-31.abstract: Kenya’s “Boda Girls” are turning a male-dominated industry upside down. Their favored customers are pregnant women needing rides to the hospital.

2025-01-31.title: Egypt Fears Syria’s Revolutionary Fervor Could Be Contagious
2025-01-31.abstract: Since Islamist rebels overthrew the Assad dictatorship in Syria, Egypt, with parallels in its own recent history, has watched warily.

2025-01-31.title: How the World Is Reeling From Trump’s Aid Freeze
2025-01-31.abstract: President Trump’s order to halt most foreign aid has intensified humanitarian crises and raised questions about the United States’ reliability as a global leader.
